返回本批次

MAUI 即将登陆 Linux

MAUI Is Coming to Linux

avaloniaui.net 作者 DeathArrow 分数 230 评论 142 2026/03/22 23:43
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 除了 Avalonia 12 和 .NET 11 预览版之外，我很高兴地宣布推出适用于 .NET MAUI 的 Avalonia 后端的首个预览版。现在，您可以利用 Avalonia 将 .NET MAUI 应用程序部署到新平台，例如 Linux 和 WebAssembly。自去年秋天以来，我们在将 Avalonia 的强大功能引入 .NET MAUI 方面取得了巨大进步。除了为 .NET MAUI 提供 Linux 和 WebAssembly 支持之外，这个新后端还推进了 Avalonia 跨平台一致性的愿景。在本机 UI 和绘制 UI 之间进行选择的理由有很多。原生化允许您的应用程序融入您的托管平台。但有时您不想要液态玻璃而更喜欢经典外观。无论您选择什么平台，我们希望这些应用程序的外观和感觉都相同。四步入门渴望立即开始？操作方法如下：创建一个 .NET MAUI 应用程序。添加 Avalonia.Controls.Maui.Desktop NuGet。添加 net11.0 目标框架。将 UseAvaloniaApp 添加到您的 MauiBuilder。就这样。运行 net11.0 目标，您的应用程序将启动。无需创建 Avalonia 引导程序；我们已经为您做到了。当然，如果您想完全控制 Avalonia 端，您可以扩展或禁用我们的源生成器。我们在存储库中提供了每种方法的示例来帮助您。 为每个人改进 Avalonia 对于我们来说，这个项目是一个很好的机会来改进 Avalonia 本身。我们希望缩小 .NET MAUI 和 Avalonia 中可用的控件集之间的差距，以避免需要实现 .NET MAUI 特定的控件。这项工作最明显的好处之一是创建了我们在 Avalonia 12 中引入的新导航 API 和控件。这些以及无数其他新功能是我们支持 .NET MAUI 工作的直接结果。使用 Avalonia 12 的任何人都可以获得全部好处，并且由于这些 .NET MAUI 处理程序是基于 Avalonia 原语构建的，因此可以通过 Avalonia API 完全自定义它们。而且，由于 Avalonia 是完全绘制的，它们在您部署到的每个平台上看起来都是一样的。 我们如何测试为了测试我们的新库，我们一直在移植现有的 .NET MAUI 应用程序并开发新的应用程序。有些您可能已经见过，例如 MauiPlanets 或我们的 2048 实现。这些应用程序在验证我们的工作方面非常有用，因为我们努力达到或超过原始 .NET MAUI 版本的同等水平。考虑到这一点，我们想尝试具有更多功能的更大规模的应用程序，看看会发生什么。以下是我们所做的一些示例：.NET MAUI 控件库这在 .NET MAUI 存储库中用于测试和演示其服务和控件。它是一个了不起的工具，可以根据本机版本检查我们的控件，看看它们的执行情况，尤其是在 WASM 等地方。AlohaAIAlohaAI 是 .NET MAUI 团队的 Jakub Florkowski 和 GitHub Copilot 之间合作创建的。该应用程序旨在通过游戏化教授大型语言模型和机器学习中的概念。凭借非常密集的 UI，涉及嵌套页面和流动动画，感觉移植的时机已经成熟。我们对底层源代码进行了微小的更改，包括添加对深色和浅色主题的支持、使其修剪安全、支持 NativeAOT 以及为导航菜单添加自定义选项卡栏。除此之外，该应用程序的结构与原始应用程序基本相同，并且它在所有 .NET MAUI 平台（本机或绘制）上同样运行良好。MyConferenceMyConference 也是由 Jakub 和 Copilot 在 .NET MAUI Live Stream 期间开发的，作为“代理 AI”开发的演示。他们能够在直播期间为会议应用程序奠定坚实的基础，在 Copilot 执行其请求时，只需 Jakub 提供有限的输入即可。这是一个巧妙的演示，我们知道我们也必须移植它。像 AlohaAI 一样，我们必须对其进行一些更改才能工作；基础应用程序有我们需要解决的主题和修剪问题。我们还需要添加 CORS 代理，以便 API 能够与 WebAssembly 配合使用。添加我们的处理程序后，一切正常。下面是在每个桌面平台上运行的应用程序，包括 Avalonia 和 .NET MAUI Native：本机还是绘制？使用本机和绘制控件运行很好地演示了 Avalonia 为 .NET MAUI 用户提供的功能。本机 .NET MAUI 版本使用操作系统的控件及其本机选项卡栏和导航页面，使其看起来与主机操作系统更加统一。同时，Avalonia.Controls.Maui 在所有平台上都具有一致的外观和行为。方法没有正确或错误之分；两者都有各自的优点，但有了 Avalonia MAUI，您现在有了更多选择，让您可以更好地控制应用程序的外观和执行方式并更加灵活。WebView WeatherTwentyOne 是一个 .NET MAUI 示例应用程序，最初是为 .NET 6 发布而开发的。它包括新颖的 UI 布局，例如使用 FlexLayout 处理侧边栏和网格。使用我们新开源的 WebView，我们创建了这个应用程序的一个端口，它在 Linux 和 WebAssembly 上运行得非常好。 GraphicsView如果您在.NET MAUI的GraphicsView或原始控件之上构建控件，那么它们很可能已经与我们的处理程序一起使用。我们一直在测试现有的库，例如 Jonathan Dick 和 Allan Ritchie 的库，它们基本上无需更改即可工作。使用 .NET MAUI 图形代码的好处是从现有 .NET MAUI 平台迁移到 Avalonia MAUI 时的无缝集成。如果您的应用程序已经依赖于它，我们的处理程序应该毫无意外地工作；它只是在新画布上绘图。 SkiaSharp.Views.Maui我们还包装了 SkiaSharp.Views.Maui 以允许依赖库与 Avalonia MAUI 进行互操作。 MapApp 通过一个简单的地图视图演示了这一点，该视图具有覆盖控件，可以在桌面上的 Avalonia 和 WASM 或 .NET MAUI Native 上运行。我们能够通过我们的处理程序系统批量使用 Mapsui.Maui 库，无需进行任何更改。展望未来 虽然自去年以来我们已经取得了长足的进步，但仍有许多领域需要解决。我们已经开始在 Avalonia 上构建 Maui.Essentials 的定制实现，并随着时间的推移扩展对更多 API 的支持。我们还计划实现与 WinUI 的互操作性，以在其中托管 Avalonia 控件，从而完成 .NET MAUI 本机平台的故事。对于针对本机平台的控件库作者，我们正在努力建立简单的模式，以允许您将控件扩展到绘制方法。我们对 .NET 11 的全面发布所取得的进展感到鼓舞。我们很高兴有人尝试 Avalonia MAUI；看看他们将应用程序带到哪里，他们尝试移植哪些新控件和库，并体验 Avalonia 所提供的功能。除了 Avalonia 12 和 .NET 11 预览版之外，我很高兴地宣布推出适用于 .NET MAUI 的 Avalonia 后端的第一个预览版。现在，您可以利用 Avalonia 将 .NET MAUI 应用程序部署到新平台，例如 Linux 和 WebAssembly。自去年秋天以来，我们在将 Avalonia 的强大功能引入 .NET MAUI 方面取得了巨大进步。除了为 .NET MAUI 提供 Linux 和 WebAssembly 支持之外，这个新后端还推进了 Avalonia 跨平台一致性的愿景。在本机 UI 和绘制 UI 之间进行选择的理由有很多。原生化允许您的应用程序融入您的托管平台。但有时您不想要液态玻璃而更喜欢经典外观。无论您选择什么平台，我们希望这些应用程序的外观和感觉都相同。四步入门渴望立即开始？操作方法如下：创建一个 .NET MAUI 应用程序。添加 Avalonia.Controls.Maui.Desktop NuGet。添加 net11.0 目标框架。将 UseAvaloniaApp 添加到您的 MauiBuilder。就这样。运行 net11.0 目标，您的应用程序将启动。无需创建 Avalonia 引导程序；我们已经为您做到了。当然，如果您想完全控制 Avalonia 端，您可以扩展或禁用我们的源生成器。我们在存储库中提供了每种方法的示例来帮助您。 为每个人改进 Avalonia 对于我们来说，这个项目是一个很好的机会来改进 Avalonia 本身。我们希望缩小 .NET MAUI 和 Avalonia 中可用的控件集之间的差距，以避免需要实现 .NET MAUI 特定的控件。这项工作最明显的好处之一是创建了我们在 Avalonia 12 中引入的新导航 API 和控件。这些以及无数其他新功能是我们支持 .NET MAUI 工作的直接结果。使用 Avalonia 12 的任何人都可以获得全部好处，并且由于这些 .NET MAUI 处理程序是基于 Avalonia 原语构建的，因此可以通过 Avalonia API 完全自定义它们。而且，由于 Avalonia 是完全绘制的，它们在您部署到的每个平台上看起来都是一样的。 我们如何测试为了测试我们的新库，我们一直在移植现有的 .NET MAUI 应用程序并开发新的应用程序。有些您可能已经见过，例如 MauiPlanets 或我们的 2048 实现。这些应用程序在验证我们的工作方面非常有用，因为我们努力达到或超过原始 .NET MAUI 版本的同等水平。考虑到这一点，我们想尝试具有更多功能的更大规模的应用程序，看看会发生什么。以下是我们所做的一些示例：.NET MAUI 控件库这在 .NET MAUI 存储库中用于测试和演示其服务和控件。它是一个了不起的工具，可以根据本机版本检查我们的控件，看看它们的执行情况，尤其是在 WASM 等地方。AlohaAIAlohaAI 是 .NET MAUI 团队的 Jakub Florkowski 和 GitHub Copilot 之间合作创建的。该应用程序旨在通过游戏化教授大型语言模型和机器学习中的概念。凭借非常密集的 UI，涉及嵌套页面和流动动画，感觉移植的时机已经成熟。我们对底层源代码进行了微小的更改，包括添加对深色和浅色主题的支持、使其修剪安全、支持 NativeAOT 以及为导航菜单添加自定义选项卡栏。除此之外，该应用程序的结构与原始应用程序基本相同，并且它在所有 .NET MAUI 平台（本机或绘制）上同样运行良好。MyConferenceMyConference 也是由 Jakub 和 Copilot 在 .NET MAUI Live Stream 期间开发的，作为“代理 AI”开发的演示。他们能够在直播期间为会议应用程序奠定坚实的基础，在 Copilot 执行其请求时，只需 Jakub 提供有限的输入即可。这是一个巧妙的演示，我们知道我们也必须移植它。像 AlohaAI 一样，我们必须对其进行一些更改才能工作；基础应用程序有我们需要解决的主题和修剪问题。我们还需要添加 CORS 代理，以便 API 能够与 WebAssembly 配合使用。添加我们的处理程序后，一切正常。下面是在每个桌面平台上运行的应用程序，包括 Avalonia 和 .NET MAUI Native：本机还是绘制？使用本机和绘制控件运行很好地演示了 Avalonia 为 .NET MAUI 用户提供的功能。本机 .NET MAUI 版本使用操作系统的控件及其本机选项卡栏和导航页面，使其看起来与主机操作系统更加统一。同时，Avalonia.Controls.Maui 在所有平台上都具有一致的外观和行为。方法没有正确或错误之分；两者都有各自的优点，但有了 Avalonia MAUI，您现在有了更多选择，让您可以更好地控制应用程序的外观和执行方式并更加灵活。WebView WeatherTwentyOne 是一个 .NET MAUI 示例应用程序，最初是为 .NET 6 发布而开发的。它包括新颖的 UI 布局，例如使用 FlexLayout 处理侧边栏和网格。使用我们新开源的 WebView，我们创建了这个应用程序的一个端口，它在 Linux 和 WebAssembly 上运行得非常好。 GraphicsView如果您在.NET MAUI的GraphicsView或原始控件之上构建控件，那么它们很可能已经与我们的处理程序一起使用。我们一直在测试现有的库，例如 Jonathan Dick 和 Allan Ritchie 的库，它们基本上无需更改即可工作。使用 .NET MAUI 图形代码的好处是从现有 .NET MAUI 平台迁移到 Avalonia MAUI 时的无缝集成。如果您的应用程序已经依赖于它，我们的处理程序应该毫无意外地工作；它只是在新画布上绘图。 SkiaSharp.Views.Maui我们还包装了 SkiaSharp.Views.Maui 以允许依赖库与 Avalonia MAUI 进行互操作。 MapApp 通过一个简单的地图视图演示了这一点，该视图具有覆盖控件，可以在桌面上的 Avalonia 和 WASM 或 .NET MAUI Native 上运行。我们能够通过我们的处理程序系统批量使用 Mapsui.Maui 库，无需进行任何更改。展望未来 虽然自去年以来我们已经取得了长足的进步，但仍有许多领域需要解决。我们已经开始在 Avalonia 上构建 Maui.Essentials 的定制实现，并随着时间的推移扩展对更多 API 的支持。我们还计划实现与 WinUI 的互操作性，以在其中托管 Avalonia 控件，从而完成 .NET MAUI 本机平台的故事。对于针对本机平台的控件库作者，我们正在努力建立简单的模式，以允许您将控件扩展到绘制方法。我们对 .NET 11 的全面发布所取得的进展感到鼓舞。我们很高兴有人尝试 Avalonia MAUI；了解他们将应用程序带到何处、尝试移植哪些新控件和库，并体验 Avalonia 所提供的功能。

Original Summary

  1. Alongside Avalonia 12 and the .NET 11 Previews, I am pleased to announce the first preview of our Avalonia backend for .NET MAUI. Now, you can leverage Avalonia to deploy .NET MAUI apps to new platforms, like Linux and WebAssembly.Since last fall, we’ve made great strides in bringing the power of Avalonia to .NET MAUI.Beyond offering Linux and WebAssembly support for .NET MAUI, this new backend advances Avalonia’s vision of cross-platform consistency. There are many great reasons to choose between native and drawn UIs. Going native allows your app to blend in with your hosted platform. But there are times when you don’t want Liquid Glass and prefer a classic look. We want these apps to look and feel the same, regardless of the platforms you choose. Get started in four stepsEager to get started right away? Here’s how:Create a .NET MAUI app.Add the Avalonia.Controls.Maui.Desktop NuGet.Add the net11.0 target framework.Add UseAvaloniaApp to your MauiBuilder.That’s it. Run the net11.0 target, and your app will launch. No need to create an Avalonia bootstrapper; we've already done that for you. Of course, you can extend or disable our source generator if you want full control on the Avalonia side. We’ve provided examples of each approach in the repository to help you.Improving Avalonia for everyoneFor us, this project was a great opportunity to introduce improvements to Avalonia itself. We wanted to close the gap between the control set available in .NET MAUI and Avalonia, to avoid needing to implement .NET MAUI-specific controls. One of the most obvious benefits of that work has been the creation of the new navigation APIs and controls we’re introducing with Avalonia 12. These, and countless other new features, are a direct result of our work supporting .NET MAUI. Anyone using Avalonia 12 gets the full benefits, and since these .NET MAUI handlers are built on Avalonia primitives, they can be fully customized through Avalonia APIs. And, thanks to Avalonia being entirely drawn, they'll look the same on every platform you deploy to.How we testedTo test our new libraries, we’ve been porting existing .NET MAUI apps and developing new ones. Some you may have already seen, such as MauiPlanets or our 2048 implementation. These apps have been extremely useful in validating our work as we strive to meet or exceed parity with the original .NET MAUI versions. With that in mind, we wanted to try larger-scale apps with more features to see what would happen. Here are some examples of what we’ve done:.NET MAUI control galleryThis is used in the .NET MAUI repository to test and demonstrate its services and controls. It has been an amazing tool for checking our controls against the native versions to see how they perform, especially in places like WASM.AlohaAIAlohaAI was created as a collaboration between Jakub Florkowski, from the .NET MAUI team, and GitHub Copilot. This app aims to teach concepts in Large Language Models and Machine Learning through gamification. With a very dense UI, involving nested pages and flowing animations, it felt ripe for porting. We made minor changes to the underlying source code, including adding support for dark and light themes, making it trim-safe, supporting NativeAOT, and adding a custom tab bar for the navigation menu. Otherwise, the app is structured largely the same as the original, and it works equally well across all .NET MAUI platforms, native or drawn.MyConferenceMyConference was developed during a .NET MAUI Live Stream, also by Jakub and Copilot, as a demonstration of "Agentic AI" development. They were able to build a solid foundation of a conference application during the stream, with limited input needed from Jakub as Copilot implemented his requests. It was a slick demo, and we knew we had to port this too.Like AlohaAI, we had to make some changes for it to work; the base app had theme and trimming issues we needed to address. We also needed to add a CORS proxy so the APIs would work with WebAssembly. After adding our handlers, everything just worked. Here's the app running on every desktop platform, with both Avalonia and .NET MAUI Native:Native or drawn?Running with both native and drawn controls is a good demonstration of what Avalonia offers .NET MAUI users. The native .NET MAUI version uses the operating system’s controls with its native tab bar and navigation pages, making it appear more unified with the host OS. Meanwhile, Avalonia.Controls.Maui has a consistent look and behavior across all platforms. There's no right or wrong approach; both have their merits, but with Avalonia MAUI, you now have options, giving you more control and flexibility over how your app looks and performs.WebView WeatherTwentyOne is a .NET MAUI sample app, originally developed for the .NET 6 launch. It includes novel UI layouts, such as handling the sidebar and grids with FlexLayout. Using our newly open-sourced WebView, we created a port of this app, which works wonderfully on Linux and WebAssembly. GraphicsViewIf you built controls on top of .NET MAUI’s GraphicsView or primitive controls, there’s a good chance they already work with our handlers. We’ve been testing existing libraries, such as those from Jonathan Dick and Allan Ritchie, and they largely work without changes. What’s great about using the .NET MAUI Graphics code is the seamless integration when moving from the existing .NET MAUI platforms to Avalonia MAUI. If your application was already dependent on it, our handlers should work with no surprises; it’s just drawing to a new canvas. SkiaSharp.Views.MauiWe’ve also wrapped SkiaSharp.Views.Maui to allow dependent libraries to interoperate with Avalonia MAUI. MapApp demonstrates this with a simple map view featuring overlaid controls that can run on Avalonia on desktop and WASM, or .NET MAUI Native. We were able to use the Mapsui.Maui library wholesale through our handler system, no changes needed.Looking ahead While we’ve come a long way since last year, there are still many areas to address. We have started work on a bespoke implementation of Maui.Essentials built on Avalonia, with expanded support for more APIs over time. We’re also planning to enable interoperability with WinUI to host Avalonia controls within it, completing the .NET MAUI native platform story. For control library authors targeting native platforms, we’re working on establishing simple patterns to allow you to extend your controls to drawn methods.We are encouraged by the progress we’ve made as we move toward the general release of .NET 11. We’re excited to have people try out Avalonia MAUI; see where they take their applications, which new controls and libraries they try to port, and experience what Avalonia has to offer.Alongside Avalonia 12 and the .NET 11 Previews, I am pleased to announce the first preview of our Avalonia backend for .NET MAUI. Now, you can leverage Avalonia to deploy .NET MAUI apps to new platforms, like Linux and WebAssembly.Since last fall, we’ve made great strides in bringing the power of Avalonia to .NET MAUI.Beyond offering Linux and WebAssembly support for .NET MAUI, this new backend advances Avalonia’s vision of cross-platform consistency. There are many great reasons to choose between native and drawn UIs. Going native allows your app to blend in with your hosted platform. But there are times when you don’t want Liquid Glass and prefer a classic look. We want these apps to look and feel the same, regardless of the platforms you choose. Get started in four stepsEager to get started right away? Here’s how:Create a .NET MAUI app.Add the Avalonia.Controls.Maui.Desktop NuGet.Add the net11.0 target framework.Add UseAvaloniaApp to your MauiBuilder.That’s it. Run the net11.0 target, and your app will launch. No need to create an Avalonia bootstrapper; we've already done that for you. Of course, you can extend or disable our source generator if you want full control on the Avalonia side. We’ve provided examples of each approach in the repository to help you.Improving Avalonia for everyoneFor us, this project was a great opportunity to introduce improvements to Avalonia itself. We wanted to close the gap between the control set available in .NET MAUI and Avalonia, to avoid needing to implement .NET MAUI-specific controls. One of the most obvious benefits of that work has been the creation of the new navigation APIs and controls we’re introducing with Avalonia 12. These, and countless other new features, are a direct result of our work supporting .NET MAUI. Anyone using Avalonia 12 gets the full benefits, and since these .NET MAUI handlers are built on Avalonia primitives, they can be fully customized through Avalonia APIs. And, thanks to Avalonia being entirely drawn, they'll look the same on every platform you deploy to.How we testedTo test our new libraries, we’ve been porting existing .NET MAUI apps and developing new ones. Some you may have already seen, such as MauiPlanets or our 2048 implementation. These apps have been extremely useful in validating our work as we strive to meet or exceed parity with the original .NET MAUI versions. With that in mind, we wanted to try larger-scale apps with more features to see what would happen. Here are some examples of what we’ve done:.NET MAUI control galleryThis is used in the .NET MAUI repository to test and demonstrate its services and controls. It has been an amazing tool for checking our controls against the native versions to see how they perform, especially in places like WASM.AlohaAIAlohaAI was created as a collaboration between Jakub Florkowski, from the .NET MAUI team, and GitHub Copilot. This app aims to teach concepts in Large Language Models and Machine Learning through gamification. With a very dense UI, involving nested pages and flowing animations, it felt ripe for porting. We made minor changes to the underlying source code, including adding support for dark and light themes, making it trim-safe, supporting NativeAOT, and adding a custom tab bar for the navigation menu. Otherwise, the app is structured largely the same as the original, and it works equally well across all .NET MAUI platforms, native or drawn.MyConferenceMyConference was developed during a .NET MAUI Live Stream, also by Jakub and Copilot, as a demonstration of "Agentic AI" development. They were able to build a solid foundation of a conference application during the stream, with limited input needed from Jakub as Copilot implemented his requests. It was a slick demo, and we knew we had to port this too.Like AlohaAI, we had to make some changes for it to work; the base app had theme and trimming issues we needed to address. We also needed to add a CORS proxy so the APIs would work with WebAssembly. After adding our handlers, everything just worked. Here's the app running on every desktop platform, with both Avalonia and .NET MAUI Native:Native or drawn?Running with both native and drawn controls is a good demonstration of what Avalonia offers .NET MAUI users. The native .NET MAUI version uses the operating system’s controls with its native tab bar and navigation pages, making it appear more unified with the host OS. Meanwhile, Avalonia.Controls.Maui has a consistent look and behavior across all platforms. There's no right or wrong approach; both have their merits, but with Avalonia MAUI, you now have options, giving you more control and flexibility over how your app looks and performs.WebView WeatherTwentyOne is a .NET MAUI sample app, originally developed for the .NET 6 launch. It includes novel UI layouts, such as handling the sidebar and grids with FlexLayout. Using our newly open-sourced WebView, we created a port of this app, which works wonderfully on Linux and WebAssembly. GraphicsViewIf you built controls on top of .NET MAUI’s GraphicsView or primitive controls, there’s a good chance they already work with our handlers. We’ve been testing existing libraries, such as those from Jonathan Dick and Allan Ritchie, and they largely work without changes. What’s great about using the .NET MAUI Graphics code is the seamless integration when moving from the existing .NET MAUI platforms to Avalonia MAUI. If your application was already dependent on it, our handlers should work with no surprises; it’s just drawing to a new canvas. SkiaSharp.Views.MauiWe’ve also wrapped SkiaSharp.Views.Maui to allow dependent libraries to interoperate with Avalonia MAUI. MapApp demonstrates this with a simple map view featuring overlaid controls that can run on Avalonia on desktop and WASM, or .NET MAUI Native. We were able to use the Mapsui.Maui library wholesale through our handler system, no changes needed.Looking ahead While we’ve come a long way since last year, there are still many areas to address. We have started work on a bespoke implementation of Maui.Essentials built on Avalonia, with expanded support for more APIs over time. We’re also planning to enable interoperability with WinUI to host Avalonia controls within it, completing the .NET MAUI native platform story. For control library authors targeting native platforms, we’re working on establishing simple patterns to allow you to extend your controls to drawn methods.We are encouraged by the progress we’ve made as we move toward the general release of .NET 11. We’re excited to have people try out Avalonia MAUI; see where they take their applications, which new controls and libraries they try to port, and experience what Avalonia has to offer.

评论（默认中文）

Ciantic 2026/03/23 01:29
我希望他们全心全意地支持Linux，很多工具包和GUI框架都是半途而废的，主要是因为Wayland很难理解。

在 Wayland 中，您有多种方式来渲染窗口，而不仅仅是 XDG 顶级窗口。它通过表面起作用，这是我迄今为止发现的列表：

  - XDG Top Level Window
  - Child Window
  - Popup Surface
  - Layer surface (like task-bars, shell overlays)
  - Subsurface (region in another surface)
  - IME Panel Surface (surface that follows text cursor)
可能还有其他人。

很难找到支持上述所有内容的高级工具包。

I wish they support Linux wholeheartedly, a lot of toolkits and GUI frameworks do it by half-assing things, mostly because Wayland is difficult to understand.

In Wayland you have multiple ways to render windows, not just the XDG top level window. It works via surfaces, and here is a list I've discovered so far:

  - XDG Top Level Window
  - Child Window
  - Popup Surface
  - Layer surface (like task-bars, shell overlays)
  - Subsurface (region in another surface)
  - IME Panel Surface (surface that follows text cursor)
There probably is others too.

It is diffifcult to find high-level toolkits that support all of the above.

在 HN 中查看
zamalek 2026/03/23 06:19
> [文章] 在 GNOME 中有效的方法可能在 KDE 中无效。在两者中都适用的方法可能不适用于 Sway。

如果你从集合中减去 GNOME，那么事情就会变得更加理智。 “特定于合成器的扩展”实际上是“除了 GNOME 扩展之外的所有扩展”。系统托盘扩展 不是 KDE 特定的。当然，窗口位置可能根本不可用（因为它们对于 TWM 没有意义），或者用户可能没有系统托盘栏（或者您可能在 GNOME 上）。但是，如果他们确实有系统托盘，那将是 StatusNotifierItem 协议。理想情况下，这些应该像其他平台功能（如加速计等）一样处理。这可能是不可能的，无论哪种方式，它们中的很多都可以安全地进行操作。

> [文章] 对于 Avalonia 来说，这意味着“Wayland 支持”不是一种实现，而是可能有数十种。我们不只是编写 Wayland 后端；我们还编写一个 Wayland 后端。我们正在编写一个 GNOME-Wayland 后端、一个 KDE-Wayland 后端、一个 Sway-Wayland 后端，

如果您正在制作 Per-WM 后端，那么您从根本上误解了扩展应该如何工作。其他 Wayland 客户端库没有 KDE、Sway 和 GNOME 的独立后端。也许需要一些怪癖，因为您正在尝试支持现有的 UI 库 - 但这些怪癖应该很少。

IIRC Avalonia 支持 Vulkan 作为渲染后端吗？ Wayland 协议与 Vulkan 扩展的思路相同。

wlroot 和 smithay 是现实世界中使用的扩展的好例子。

> [Article] What works in GNOME might not work in KDE. What works in both might not work in Sway.

If you subtract GNOME from the set then things become a lot more sane. "Compositor-specific extensions" are really "everyone besides GNOME extensions." The system tray extension isn't KDE-specific. Sure, window positions might not be available at all (because they don't make sense for a TWM), or a user might not have a system tray bar (or you might be on GNOME). However, if they did have a system tray it would be the StatusNotifierItem protocol. Ideally, these should be handled like other platform features like accelerometers etc.. That may not be possible, either way a lot of them can safely noop.

> [Article] For Avalonia, this means "Wayland support" isn't one implementation, it's potentially dozens. We're not just writing a Wayland backend; we're writing a GNOME-Wayland backend, a KDE-Wayland backend, a Sway-Wayland backend,

If you're making per-WM backends then you've fundamentally misunderstood how extensions are supposed to work. Other Wayland client libraries do not have a independent backends for KDE, Sway, and GNOME. Maybe quirks would be needed because you're attempting to support an existing UI library - but those should be few and far between.

IIRC Avalonia supports Vulkan as a rendering backend? Wayland protocols are the same line of thinking as Vulkan extensions.

wlroot and smithay are good examples of what extensions are used in the real world.

在 HN 中查看
flohofwoe 2026/03/23 17:48
Fwiw 在 Vulkan 或 GL 上，为受支持的扩展的不同“集”实现完全不同的代码路径通常也是有意义的，而不是在单个代码路径中处理所有可能的扩展组合（这会导致混乱的代码和测试用例的组合爆炸，因为理论上每个唯一的扩展可能独立于所有其他扩展而受支持或不受支持 - 但您需要测试每个组合）。

在 GL、Vulkan 和 Wayland 中，细粒度和独立扩展的整个想法是相当愚蠢的。拥有少量“层”或“配置文件”来保证一组特定的功能（例如所有其他 3D API 如何做到这一点）更有意义 - 例如Wayland 应该有一个“桌面配置文件”，而不是桌面场景所需的数十个可选扩展，其他配置文件可以是“移动”和“信息亭”。

Fwiw on Vulkan or GL it also often makes sense to implement entirely different code paths for different *sets* of supported extensions, instead of handling all possible extension combinations in a single code path (which leads to messy code and a combinatorial explosion of test cases, since each unique extension might theoretically be supported or unsupported independently from all other extensions - but you need to test each combination).

The whole idea of granular and independent extensions is pretty stupid across GL, Vulkan and Wayland. It makes more sense to have a handful "tiers" or "profiles" which guarantee a specific set of features (eg how all other 3D APIs do it) - e.g. Wayland should have a "desktop profile" instead of dozens of optional extensions needed for desktop scenarios, other profiles could be "mobile" and "kiosk".

a_vanderbilt 2026/03/23 11:56
我在帮助 Steam Deck 游戏适应更广泛的 Linux 支持时也有同样的经历。问题不在于韦兰主义，其中大多数已经解决了。这是 GNOME。他们对问题的首选解决方案似乎是放弃支持而不是修复错误，并且他们不遗余力地采用与更广泛社区的势头背道而驰的实现。我可以理解他们为什么做出一些决定，但是诸如取消托盘指示器或服务器端装饰之类的事情是疯狂的。为了更大或更宏大的目标而成为一个局外人是一回事，然后就是 GNOME 正在做的事情。
My experience was the same while helping to adapt a Steam Deck game for wider Linux support. The issue wasn't Waylandisms, most of those have already by figured out. It was GNOME. Their preferred resolution to issues seems to be dropping support rather than bug fixes, and they go out of their way to adopt implementations that are against the momentum of the wider community. I can get why they make some of their decisions, but things like killing the tray indicator or server side decorations are insane. To be an outlier in name of a greater or grander goal is one thing, then there is whatever GNOME is doing.
在 HN 中查看
kelnos 2026/03/23 08:50
> 对于 Avalonia 来说，这意味着“Wayland 支持”不是一种实现，而是可能有数十种。我们不只是编写 Wayland 后端；我们还编写一个 Wayland 后端。我们正在编写一个 GNOME-Wayland 后端、一个 KDE-Wayland 后端、一个 Sway-Wayland 后端等等。

什么？ 不，事实并非如此。 是的，不同的 Wayland 合成器通常支持不同的 扩展，但每个人都有基础知识（Wayland core、xdg_shell，可能还有其他一些）。 您需要一个后端，然后您可以支持扩展来实现更高级的功能，但您当然必须能够在没有任何扩展的情况下继续工作。

是的，有些功能在 GNOME 上可能需要与 KDE 上不同的扩展（例如），但您不需要完整的“后端”来处理这些差异。

作为一个一直对 Wayland 持怀疑态度、对其缺点感到沮丧的人，以及为 Wayland 编写了一个合成器和类似 XEMBED 的库的人，感觉作者只是为了公关目的而试图在这里夸大其困难。

> For Avalonia, this means "Wayland support" isn't one implementation, it's potentially dozens. We're not just writing a Wayland backend; we're writing a GNOME-Wayland backend, a KDE-Wayland backend, a Sway-Wayland backend, and so on.

What? No, that's not the case. Yes, different Wayland compositors often support different extensions, but everyone has the basics (Wayland core, xdg_shell, and probably a few others). You need one backend, and then you can support extensions to implement more advanced features, but you of course have to be able to continue to work without any extensions present.

Yes, there are some features that might require a different extension on GNOME than it does on KDE (for example), but you don't need a full "backend" to handle those differences.

As someone who has always been skeptical of Wayland, frustrated with its shortcomings, and who has written both a compositor and XEMBED-workalike library for Wayland, it just feels like author is trying to play up the difficulties for PR purposes here.

thayne 2026/03/23 14:15
> 有些功能在 GNOME 上可能需要与 KDE 上不同的扩展名

更有可能的是，您必须使用 GNOME 扩展（如在插件中，而不是 wayland 扩展），或者可能是 gnome 特定的、可能未记录的 D-bus 协议。

> there are some features that might require a different extension on GNOME than it does on KDE

More likely you have to use a GNOME Extension (as in the plugin, not a wayland extension), or maybe a gnome-specific, possibly undocumented D-bus protocol.

throwaway27448 2026/03/23 08:26
为什么 Wayland 如此复杂？我认为放弃 X11 的一半原因是为了生产一个更简单的窗口服务器。当我意识到存在竞争的合成器似乎对任何人都没有好处时，我大吃一惊。
Why is Wayland so complicated? I thought half the reason for breaking with X11 was to produce a simpler window server. I was flabbergasted when I realized that there were competing compositors for seemingly no benefit to anyone.
在 HN 中查看
sho_hn 2026/03/23 14:54
> 为什么 Wayland 如此复杂？

它并不是特别复杂，而且几乎在各个方面都比 X11 简单和干净得多。

现实情况是，围绕这个话题存在着一种令人憎恶的一知半解的暴徒动态，人们遇到一个错误，进行一些在线搜索，遇到现有的暴徒，复制它的一些“论点”，整个事情不断滚动和滚雪球。

有时这是天真的，就像OP发现UI实际上并不平凡，并且有不同类型的窗口用于不同的事情（就像在任何生产级窗口系统中一样）。因此，他们以列表的形式分享新发现的知识。然后暴徒走过来说：“看看这个！他们有一份清单，可能太长了！”然后在下一个讨论中，“你知道 Wayland 有一个很长的清单吗？！”等等。

这就像政治一样，是循环往复的。有一天，叙述将再次发生转变。

就这一点而言，暴民也不会相信我，但是 FWIW，我在 Linux 桌面上工作了 20 多年，作为 KDE Plasma 任务栏的首席开发人员和其窗口管理器的半定期贡献者，我以世界上相对较少的人的方式接触到这些系统的复杂性。我宁愿保留 Wayland 代码，也不愿保留 X11 代码，因为我写了很多 X11 代码。

> Why is Wayland so complicated?

It's not particularly complicated, and certainly a lot simpler and cleaner than X11 in almost every way.

The reality of the situation is that there's sort of a hateful half-knowledge mob dynamic around the topic, where people run into a bug, do some online search, run into the existing mob, copy over some of its "arguments" and the whole thing keeps rolling and snowballing.

Sometimes this innocent, like OP discovering that UIs are actually non-trivial and there's different types of windows for different things (like in really any production-grade windowing system). So they share their new-found knowledge in the form of a list. Then the mob comes along and goes "look at this! they have a list of things, and it's probably too long!" and then in the next discussion it's "Did you know that Wayland has a LONG LIST OF THINGS?!" and so on and so forth.

It's like politics, and it's cyclic. One day the narrative will shift again.

The mob will not believe me either, for that matter, but FWIW, I've worked on the Linux desktop for over 20 years, and as the lead developer of KDE Plasma's taskbar and a semi-regular contributor to its window manager, I'm exposed to the complexity of these systems in a way that only relatively few people in the world are. And I'd rather keep the Wayland code than the X11 one, which I wrote a lot of.

flohofwoe 2026/03/23 20:02
> 现实情况是，围绕这个主题有一种可恨的一知半解的暴民动态，人们遇到错误，进行一些在线搜索，遇到现有的暴民，复制它的一些“论点”，整个事情不断滚动和滚雪球。

大多数其他 Linux 项目“正常运行”，没有任何戏剧性（通常不是源自 Red Hat 的项目？）。让您想知道为什么 Wayland 如此特别（或者可能是红帽公司文化的特别之处？）。

有时，一个设计糟糕的系统就是一个设计糟糕的系统，当涉及到用户和应用程序开发人员的反馈时，Wayland 背后的主要力量似乎异常充耳不闻和防御性（例如，似乎普遍存在“我们更知道什么对你有好处”的态度）。

> The reality of the situation is that there's sort of a hateful half-knowledge mob dynamic around the topic, where people run into a bug, do some online search, run into the existing mob, copy over some of its "arguments" and the whole thing keeps rolling and snowballing.

Most other Linux projects "just work" without any drama (usually those not originating at Red Hat?). Makes you wonder why Wayland is so special (or maybe it is something special about the Red Hat company culture?).

Sometimes a badly designed system is simply a badly designed system, and the main forces behind Wayland seem to be exceptionally tone deaf and defensive when it comes to feedback both from users and application developers (e.g. there seems to be a general "we know better what's good for you" attitude).

torginus 2026/03/23 19:20
这就是我不喜欢很多开源项目（不是全部，但其中很大一部分是著名的）的原因 - 公开宣称代码是开放的，您可以自由地修复错误并实施更改。

但是当您尝试这样做时，您会发现公开的文档和帮助非常少，并且通过提交历史记录，您可以看到大约有三打人曾经为该项目做出过贡献。

代码更改的速度和晦涩难懂意味着知识根本不会随着时间的推移而积累。

因此，尚不清楚如何解决您遇到的问题或添加功能，以及如果您这样做了，如何合并您的更改。

This is what I dislike about a lot of open source projects (not all, but a significant amout of the notable ones) - publicly it's proclaimed that the code's open, and you are free to fix bugs and implement your changes.

But when you try to do so, you see there's very little documentation and help out in the open, and by the commit history, you can see there's been like three dozen people who have ever contributed to the project.

The rate of code change and the obscurity means the knowledge simply does not build up over time.

So it's unclear how to fix an issue you've encountered, or add a feature, and if you've done so, how to get your changes merged.

kelnos 2026/03/23 08:54
wayland.app 只是 HTML 呈现规范 XML 文件的内容。 如果合成器或客户端没有像 wayland.app 所说的那样解释可空性，那么这就是合成器或客户端中的错误。
wayland.app just HTML-renders the contents of the specification XML files. If a compositor or client is not interpreting nullability the same way wayland.app says it should be interpreted, then that's a bug in the compositor or client.

在 HN 中查看
audidude 2026/03/23 03:01
在 X11 中，我们通过提供以下功能使事情变得简单：

* 核心协议绘图（直线、矩形、弧线、经典）

* 用于合成和 Alpha 的 XRender

* XShm 用于共享内存 blit

* GLX 如果您想带 GPU 参加 2D 战斗

* XVideo 用于覆盖视频路径

* Pixmap 与 Windows，因为当您可以拥有两个略有不同的可绘制对象时，为什么还要使用一个可绘制对象

* 当然，如果您喜欢将延迟作为设计约束，则可以通过网络进行间接渲染

In X11 we kept things simple by offering:

* Core protocol drawing (lines, rectangles, arcs, the classics)

* XRender for compositing and alpha

* XShm for shared-memory blits

* GLX if you felt like bringing a GPU to a 2D fight

* XVideo for overlay video paths

* Pixmaps vs Windows, because why have one drawable when you can have two subtly different ones

* And of course, indirect rendering over the network if you enjoy latency as a design constraint

And it worked very well for a remarkably long time. Even over dialup if you were patient. It still seems bizarre that the security flaws couldn’t be addressed, I never understood the Wayland push and I still don’t.
在 HN 中查看
sho_hn 2026/03/23 17:32
> 我从来不理解 Wayland 的推动，现在仍然不理解。

发生的事情基本上是这样的：

- X11 相当复杂，有很多无用的代码，以及一大堆基本问题，这些问题正在积累起来，以保证“X12”并破坏核心协议来得到修复。

- 除此之外，在相对遥远的过去的某个时刻，XFree86 实现（当时最常用的实现，后来产生了 Xorg）非常庞大并且做了大量的事情，包括驱动程序级别的工作 - 想想“用户空间中的 PCI”。

- 多年来，越来越多的工作从 X 服务器转移到例如服务器上。 Linux 内核。驱动程序、模式设置、输入事件处理、更高级别的输入事件处理（libinput）。 Xorg 也变得更加简洁和模块化，使得一些剩余的硬位以库的形式可用（例如 xkb）。

- 现在，窗口系统所需的几乎所有内容都被干净地分解出来，并且不再在 X 中，尝试全新的窗口系统突然变成了一个容易处理的问题。这使得 Wayland 的原始作者能够尝试并提交给其他人评估。

- 许多活跃的 X 和 GUI 开发人员喜欢他们所看到的内容，并且它成功地流行起来。

- 事实上，很多参与其中的人并不天真，并没有忽视经典的“应该迭代还是重新开始”的难题。 Wayland 很早就以 Xwayland 的形式有一个相当强大的向后兼容故事，它几乎立即被创建，并说服了很多人。

最后这是一个非常经典的软件工程故事。是否有可能将 X11 迭代到 Wayland 现在的样子？或许。没有把握。最终的结果会很像今天的 Wayland 吗？也许，Wayland 的设计还是相当不错和现代的。

最终这很像 Python 2.x 与 3.x 的对比。

> I never understood the Wayland push and I still don’t.

What happened is basically this:

- X11 was fairly complex, had a lot of dead-weight code, and a whole bunch of fundamental issues that were building up to warrant an "X12" and breaking the core protocols to get fixed.

- Alongside this, at some point in the relatively distant past, the XFree86 implementation (the most used one at the time, which later begat Xorg) was massively fat and did a huge amount of stuff, including driver-level work - think "PCI in userspace".

- Over the years, more and more of the work moved out of the X server and into e.g. the Linux kernel. Drivers, mode setting, input event handling, higher-level input event handling (libinput). Xorg also got a bit cleaner and modularized, making some of the remaining hard bits available in library form (e.g. xkb).

- With almost everything you need for a windowing system now cleanly factored out and no longer in X, trying out a wholly new windowing system suddenly became a tractable problem. This enabled the original Wayland author to attempt it and submit it for others to appraise.

- A lot of the active X and GUI developer liked what they saw and it managed to catch on.

- A lot of the people involved were in fact not naive, and did not ignore the classic "should you iterate or start over" conundrum. Wayland had a fairly strong backward compat story very early on in the form of Xwayland, which was created almost immediately, and convinced a lot of people.

In the end this is a very classic software engineering story. Would it have been possible to iterate X11 toward what Wayland is now in-place? Maybe. Not sure. Would the end result look a lot like Wayland today? Probably, the Wayland design is still quite good and modern.

It's a lot like Python 2.x vs. 3.x in the end.

在 HN 中查看
gf000 2026/03/23 18:44
它从来都没有很好地工作过——在位图时代（这不是最近的发明），超愚蠢的像素推动根本不再缩放。

我们有无数更好的方法来传输图形缓冲区（另外，声音呢？这似乎是合理的 - 但现在应该 展示 服务器也是一个健全的服务器？），因此将其构建到这样的核心协议中是没有意义的。

It never worked very well - in the age of bitmaps (and that's not a recent invention) that ultra-dumb pixel pushing simply no longer scales.

We have eons better ways to transport graphical buffers around (also, what about sound? That seems reasonable - but now should the display server also be a sound server?), so building it into such a core protocol just doesn't make sense.

在 HN 中查看
UltraSane 2026/03/23 12:20
一旦应用程序移至本地并且 GPU 成为渲染路径，X11 的网络透明度就成为 99% 用户的纯粹开销。 Wayland 通过将共享内存缓冲区作为核心原语并将远程访问作为单独的关注点来解决这个问题。
Once applications moved local and GPUs became the rendering path X11's network transparency became pure overhead for 99% of users. Wayland fixes this by making shared-memory buffers the core primitive and remote access a separate concern.
在 HN 中查看
whizzter 2026/03/23 14:22
更不用说一旦共享缓冲区发挥作用，X11 的复杂性就会飙升。

X11 在当时来说还不错，但从根本上来说，它是一种非常过时的设计，无法以当时解决问题的方式通过网络解决 80 年代/90 年代的问题。

Not to mention that the complexity of X11 shots through the roof once shared buffers comes into play.

X11 was ok for it's time, but fundamentally it's an really outdated design to solve 80s/90s issues over the network in the way you solved it back then.

在 HN 中查看
UltraSane 2026/03/23 18:27
它已经过时得令人难以置信，并且即使在没有网络的情况下，也强制所有图形都通过蹩脚的 80 年代网络协议流动。它是图形技术的喉返神经。
It is INCREDIBLY outdated and forces all graphics to flow through a crappy 80s era network protocol even when there is no network. It is the recurrent laryngeal nerve of graphics technology.

flohofwoe 2026/03/23 18:04
如果 Wayland 不需要网络透明性，为什么 Wayland 客户端和“服务器”仍然通过套接字进行通信？

为什么不使用更简单的命令缓冲区模型（如 3D API），并且仅用于实际异步命令（而不是简单的同步 getter）？

PS：io_uring 是否使用套接字作为其“共享内存环形缓冲区”？如果不是为什么？开销太大？如果有显着的套接字开销，为什么 Wayland 使用套接字（因为它必须解决与 io_uring 或 3D API 类似的问题）。

If Wayland doesn't need network transparency why do Wayland clients and "server" still communicate through a socket though?

Why not use a much simpler command buffer model like on 3D APIs, and only for actually asynchronous commands (but not simple synchronous getters)?

PS: is io_uring using sockets for its "shared memory ringbuffers"? If not why? Too much overhead? And if there's notable socket overhead, why is Wayland using sockets (since it has to solve a simar problem as io_uring or 3D APIs).

在 HN 中查看
gf000 2026/03/23 18:47
不过，它仅使用套接字将句柄传递给缓冲区。套接字速度非常快，但首先不移动东西是无法被击败的。
It's only using sockets to pass a handle to a buffer though. Sockets are plenty fast, but not moving stuff in the first place can't be beaten.

flohofwoe 2026/03/23 19:43
> 在任何地方使用 GTK（或 Qt）不是更容易吗？

其中哪一个？ GTK 应用程序在 KDE 桌面上看起来很陌生，而 Qt 应用程序在 GNOME 桌面上看起来很陌生。此外，如果您只需要创建一个带有 GL 或 Vulkan 画布的窗口，那么引入整个 UI 框架依赖项就太过分了。有 SDL、GLFW、winit 等 - 但这些也不能解决所有情况下的“本机窗口镶边”问题，并且它们都必须解决缺少的 Wayland 功能。裸窗口系统功能（管理窗口 - 包括窗口镶边和定位（！）、剪贴板、拖放等）确实应该成为操作系统 API 的一部分（就像在*所有其他*桌面操作系统上一样）。为什么桌面 Linux 必须做自己的事情，而且比其他桌面操作系统更糟糕（从某种意义上说：对开发人员更具敌意）？

> Wouldn't it just be easier to use GTK (or Qt) everywhere?

Which of those? GTK apps look alien on KDE desktops, and Qt apps look alien on GNOME desktops. Also, if you only need to create a window with a GL or Vulkan canvas, pulling in an entire UI framework dependency is overkill. There's SDL, GLFW, winit etc etc - but those also don't fix the 'native window chrome' problem in all situations and they all have to work around missing Wayland features. The bare window system functionality (managing windows - including window chrome and positioning(!), clipboard, drag'n'drop, ...) should really be part of the OS APIs (like it is on *every other* desktop operating system). Why does desktop Linux have to do its own thing, and worse (in the sense of: more developer hostile) than other desktop operating systems?

anthk 2026/03/23 15:58
与 Windows 没有太大区别：

- 为了让现代 DX 和旧式 Direct Draw 游戏能够在 Windows 8 及更高版本中快速运行，它们常常是一团乱麻。

- Windows 的 XVideo 和覆盖视频 100% 相同；绘图时出现绿色故障，屏幕截图时出现蓝色屏幕等。

- Windows 中使用像素图也存在同样的问题。

- RDP 在那里很好，甚至 Gnome 也采用了它。但我更喜欢 9front 的透明度，你不需要从主机获取所有东西来使用它，使用 9front 你可以只获取你想要的设备，你可以将身份验证与 GUI 等解耦，甚至是网络和音频设备。比 X11 的远程设置、VNC、RDP 等等束缚得多。

Not very different from Windows:

- Modern DX and old Direct Draw games often was a clusterfuck in order to keep them running fast in Windows 8 and up.

- XVideo and overlay video for Windows were 100% the same; green glitches on drawing, blue screens on screnshots et all.

- Same issue in Windows with pixmaps.

- RDP was fine there, even Gnome adopted it. But I prefer 9front's transparency, you don't need to get everything from the host to use it, with 9front you can just get the device you want, you can decouple auth from the GUI and a lot more, even the network and audio devices. Much fetter than X11's remote setups, VNC, RDP and whatnot.

9front is nice, but I'm not sure how much you'd get out of it without implementing the rest of Plan 9 in the target OS.

About two years ago I tried to switch, then gave up when I realised how many things are missing on wayland. And then I noticed that barely anyone wrote software for wayland. It feels like a corporate advertisement project really. GNOME and KDE push for wayland now.

What does “nobody writes for Wayland” mean?

If you write software using GTK, Qt, or FLTK then you are writing Wayland software.

The majority of Linux desktops are Wayland at this point. Nobody writes software for them?

The Steamdeck uses gamescope which is Wayland. GNOME, COSMIC, Budgie, Niri, and Hyprland are not just Wayland but Wayland only. KDE will be Wayland only soon. Cinnamon is switching to Wayland. XFCE is writing a Wayland compositor.

What percentage of Linux desktop users are not using one of the above? 10 at most?

flohofwoe 2026/03/23 19:56
通过 Xlib 创建窗口和 Vulkan 上下文并接收输入事件（例如，类似游戏的应用程序需要什么）相当简单，但在 Wayland 中则要差得多。与 Win32 或 Cocoa 相比，Xlib API 也很糟糕，但是为创建和管理窗口这样琐碎的事情编写 Wayland 客户端比在任何其他平台上都要糟糕得多（请参阅： https://www.p4m.dev/posts/29/index.html）
Creating a window and Vulkan context and receiving input events (e.g. what game-like applications need) is fairly trivial via Xlib but far worse in Wayland. The Xlib API also sucks compared to Win32 or Cocoa, but writing a Wayland client for something as trivial as creating and managing a window is much worse than on any other platform (see: https://www.p4m.dev/posts/29/index.html)

sprash 2026/03/23 07:12
这是/基本上是相同的做法 除了 对于真正重要的情况。一般的窗口管理器、xdotool、各种输入映射和自动化或者您可以用不到 500 个 LOC 编写屏幕截图工具的事实只有在您直接与 X 对话时才可能实现。那里的可能性几乎是无限的。多亏了 xcb 库，它实际上使用起来有点方便。

此外，某些类型的 Mac 电动工具可能也需要直接使用 Quartz。

It was/is mostly the same practice except for the cases where it really counts. Window managers in general, xdotool, all kinds of input mapping and automation or the fact that you can write a screenshot tool in less than 500 LOC are only possible if you talk to X directly. And there the possibilities are almost limitless. And thanks to the xcb library it's actually somewhat convenient to use.

Also certain types of power tools for mac probably need use Quartz directly as well.

在 HN 中查看
kelnos 2026/03/23 09:02
> 一般的窗口管理器、xdotool、各种输入映射和自动化或者您可以用不到 500 个 LOC 编写屏幕截图工具的事实只有在您直接与 X 对话时才可能实现。

老实说，我认为这是“没有人”的相当公平的近似。 有多少人在编写这样的工具，而有多少人在编写常规应用程序？ 我想说，这个数字非常小。

在直接广泛使用 libX11/libxcb 和 libwayland-client 后，我​​可以说它们中没有一个是特别令人愉快的。 事实上，不，这不是真的：libwayland-client 轻松获胜。 每个 Wayland 协议都有为其生成的代码，其工作方式完全相同。 我想 libxcb 也是如此（或多或少），但 libX11（以及您可能必须使用的所有其他库，如 libXrender、libXrandr、libXext...）完全是一团糟。

即便如此，libwayland-client 的数量也要少得多 概念 你必须比 libxcb 更理解，因为 Wayland 协议有少量你需要理解的概念。 libxcb 绝对是对 libX11 的改进，但它并不能神奇地使所有底层 X11 协议概念变得统一。

> Window managers in general, xdotool, all kinds of input mapping and automation or the fact that you can write a screenshot tool in less than 500 LOC are only possible if you talk to X directly.

Honestly I think this is a pretty fair approximation of "no one". How many people are writing tools like this vs. the number of people writing regular applications? A very small number, I'd say.

And after working extensively with both libX11/libxcb and libwayland-client directly, I can say that none of them are particularly pleasant to work with. Actually, no, that's not true: libwayland-client wins, easily. Every single Wayland protocol has code generated for it that works exactly the same way. I suppose the same is (more or less) true of libxcb, but libX11 (and all the other libraries you might have to use, like libXrender, libXrandr, libXext...) are a complete mess.

And even then, libwayland-client has a much lower number of concepts you have to understand than libxcb does, simply because the Wayland protocol has a small number of concepts you need to understand. libxcb is definitely an improvement over libX11, but it can't magically make all the underlying X11 protocol concepts become unified.

gf000 2026/03/23 18:52
为什么我们要针对“我可以在多少个位置编写屏幕截图工具”进行优化？我更愿意拥有 1-2 个具有所有必要功能的体面产品，就到此为止了。然后他们可以有一个简单的 API 这样你就可以编写任何你想要的脚本。

为什么我要增加显示服务器的复杂性？

And why should we optimize for "how many locs can I write a screenshot tool in"? I would prefer to having 1-2 decent ones that have all the necessary features and call it a day. They can then have a simple API so you can script whatever you want.

Why would I want to add more complexity to a display server?

hparadiz 2026/03/23 07:24
最近编写了一个新的 gtk 程序，我必须在代码中实现 Wayland 和 X -isms。我突然想到 X 更喜欢使用 W_Class，而 Wayland 更喜欢 app-id，每个窗口都有一个角色集。两者都很好。老实说，我认为 Wayland 更好，但实际上你为两者编写代码。要获得全局热键，您可以在合成器中注册它并且它可以工作。但如果您确实愿意，可以运行后台守护程序来捕获所有键。
Having written a new gtk program recently I had to implement Wayland and X -isms in the code. Off the top of my head X prefers using W_Class and Wayland prefers app-id with each window having a role set. Both are fine. I honestly think Wayland is nicer but realistically you code for both. To get a global hot key you register it with the compositor and it works. But you can run a background daemon to catch all keys if you really want.

No one who loved themselves wrote Xlib apps directly either.

whizzter 2026/03/23 14:34
我其实直接写了一些东西，因为我年轻、贫穷、愚蠢。

在大学的第一年，我的 Windows 笔记本电脑坏了，不得不拖着沉重的二手动力不足的 ppc powerbook 并编写了一些我需要的应用程序，我不想“臃肿”。

正如我在另一条评论中所写的，字体处理、共享内存后备缓冲区、网络 api 等。这是一个 API 在 80 年代末/90 年代初使用当时的习语来解决线图形的问题，到了 2000 年，问题（光栅化能力）已经不存在，甚至不是一个合适的 API 表面（25 年后更是如此）。

I actually wrote some stuff directly because I was young, poor and stupid.

First year in uni my windows laptop broke, had to lug around a heavy second hand underpowered ppc powerbook and wrote some application I needed that I didn't want "bloated".

Font handling, shared memory backbuffers, network api, etc.. as I wrote in another comment. It is an API to solve over the wire graphics in the late 80s/early 90s era using idioms of that time, and already by year 2000 the problems (rasterization power) didn't exist nor is it even a suitable API surface (even less so 25 years later).

sprash 2026/03/23 07:03
这意味着 Wayland 是有史以来最糟糕、最愚蠢的图形 API。直接谈论 Wayland 的“异步面向对象协议”简直就是一场灾难 [1]。为了完成像截屏这样简单的事情，需要以 dbus 密集的“桌面门户”迷宫形式存在的辅助和部分重复的基础设施。

因此，它本质上巩固了 GTK/Qt 双头垄断。两者都是质量极其低劣且臃肿的工具包，这也是导致 Linux 桌面在 2026 年仍然不存在的原因。

1.: https://www.p4m.dev/posts/29/index.html

It means that Wayland is the worst and most idiotic graphics API ever conceived. Talking to Wayland's "asynchronous object oriented protocol" directly is a fucking disaster [1]. Secondary and partly duplicated infrastructure in form of a dbus infested maze of "Desktop-Portals" is necessary to do things as simple as taking screenshots.

As such it essentially cements the GTK/Qt duopoly. Both are extremely subpar low-quality bloated toolkits that are also responsible for the fact that the Linux desktop is still not a thing in 2026.

1.: https://www.p4m.dev/posts/29/index.html

在 HN 中查看
grekand46 2026/03/23 08:56
Wayland 并不是真正的图形 API，它只是客户端与合成器通信的协议。当然，有很多样板可以在屏幕上显示窗口，但如果您想要超级简单的东西，您也不应该直接使用它。它实际上是一个低级接口，供在其上构建的工具包使用。以下是我对链接文章的两个不同意见：

1.与raylib的比较

在我看来，这是苹果与橙子的比较。 Raylib 的层次比 wayland 高得多，它实际上支持使用 wayland 作为后端。

2. Wayland 充满了回调，因为它是一个面向对象的协议

这更多是因为 Wayland 是一个异步协议。当您向合成器发送请求时，您很可能不会立即收到回复。但应用程序也可能在等待响应时执行其他操作。 X11 实际上也是一个异步协议，只是 XLib 在其之上创建了一个同步 API （因此会遭受不必要的往返延迟）。相比之下，较新的 XCB 库在保留协议的异步性质方面更加忠实，并且被 Qt 的 X11 后端甚至 XLib 本身等使用。当然，这也使得它更难以使用，与 libwayland 不同，但这里的主要要点是，如果您愿意，您可以在异步之上构建同步 API ，反之亦然。

我认为作者的某些部分的挫败感是错误的，因为他们将 libwayland 视为一个工具包，在这种情况下，是的，这是非常痛苦的。但我真的不同意这样的结论：这在某种程度上使它成为一个糟糕的基础。打个比方，对内核进行原始系统调用也是痛苦的，但这就是库存在的原因。

（编辑以获得更好的格式）

Wayland isn't really a graphics API, it's just a protocol for clients to communicate with the compositor. Sure, there's a lot of boilerplate to get a window on the screen, but you also aren't supposed to use it directly if you want something super simple. It's really meant to be a low level interface for toolkits to be built on top of. Here are two disagreements I have with the linked article:

1. Comparison with raylib

This is imo comparing apples with oranges. Raylib sits at a much higher level than wayland, and it in fact supports using wayland as a backend.

2. Wayland is littered with callbacks because it's an object oriented protocol

It's more due to wayland being an asynchronous protocol. When you send a request to the compositor, chances are that you wont hear back from it immediately. But it's also likely that the app can do other things while waiting for the response. X11 is also in fact an asynchronous protocol, it's just that XLib creates a synchronous API on top of it (and as a result suffers from unnecessary roundtrip delays). In comparison, the newer XCB library is a lot more faithful in terms of preserving the asynchronous nature of the protocol and is used by, for example, Qt's X11 backend and even XLib itself. Of course that also makes it more difficult to use, not unlike libwayland, but the main takeaway here is that you can build a sync API on top of an async one if you wish and not vice versa.

I think some parts of the author's frustration is misplaced because they see libwayland as a toolkit, and in that case yeah it's pretty painful. But I really don't agree with the conclusion that this somehow makes it a bad foundation to build upon. As an analogy, making raw syscalls to the kernel is also painful, but that's why libraries exist.

(edit for better formatting)

在 HN 中查看
hparadiz 2026/03/23 07:26
设置权限后，屏幕截图程序始终拥有这些权限，并且您不必多次授予权限。
Once you set permissions the screenshot taking program always has them and you don't have to do grants more than once.
在 HN 中查看
kelnos 2026/03/23 09:21
我同意门户网站的情况是一团糟，但除非您是沙盒应用程序，否则您不需要它们来截屏。 这可能是一件好事。

您可以使用几百行代码[0]编写一个简单的 Wayland 屏幕截图应用程序，以及一个支持 ext-image-capture 源和已实现的 ext-image-copy-capture 扩展的合成器。 （或者旧的 wlr-screencopy。）

我有 足够 对 Wayland 及其生态系统的批评，但如果您要批评，请不要传播 FUD。

（我不喜欢成为那个必须证明自己的凭据的人，但是：大约一个月前，我已经在 Wayland 合成器中实现了所有这三个屏幕截图/截屏协议，并且知道它们是如何工作的，以及如何与客户交谈。）

我还阅读了您发布的链接。 这些挫败感有很多道理，但很多都是基于对 Wayland 实际情况的误解。 是的，大多数人应该使用工具包。 不，主要选择是 GTK 和 Qt，这不太好。 我认为中级工具包绝对有空间，可以让您完成基础操作，而不需要所有 Wayland 样板。 smithay-client-toolkit 就是这样的一个努力，我认为这是一个好的开始，尽管在它之上有更高级别的东西会更好。

我也不讨厌回调。 我更喜欢注册回调，而不是一个巨大的 switch 语句，后者必须在阳光下分派每个事件。 工具包也使用回调；作者是否也讨厌所有工具包？ 如果你愿意的话，你实际上可以用一个大的 switch 语句与 Wayland 合成器对话，尽管你需要修改 libwayland-client 以在迭代其事件队列时返回事件，而不是将事件分派给回调。 对于那些想要像 libX11 事件处理一样处理 Wayland 事件的人来说，这可能是一个有趣的项目。 （参见：这只是对 Wayland 的根本误解。）

将 raylib 与 libwayland-client 进行比较是愚蠢的；它们是根本不同的东西。 您可以使用 raylib 与 Wayland 合成器对话。 这只是一种恶意的争论。

如果你想将 libwayland-client 与某些东西进行比较，你必须将它与 libX11 或 libxcb 进行比较。 虽然是的，使用 libX11/libxcb 在屏幕上显示一个简单的窗口确实更简单，但做任何比这更复杂的事情都与使用 libwayland-client 最终做的事情相当。

[0] 不计算 wayland-scanner 将为您生成的协议代码，因为这就像说您必须计算 libX11 中的代码行数才能编写 X11 屏幕截图应用程序。

I agree the portals thing is a horrid mess, but you don't need them to take a screenshot unless you're a sandboxed application. Which is probably a good thing.

You can write a simple Wayland screenshot app with a few hundred lines of code[0], and a compositor that supports the ext-image-capture source and ext-image-copy-capture extensions implemented. (Or the older wlr-screencopy.)

I have plenty of criticism for Wayland and its ecosystem, but if you're going to criticize, don't spread FUD.

(I don't like being the guy who has to assert his credentials, but: I've implemented all three of those screenshot/screencast protocols in a Wayland compositor, just a month or so ago, and know how they work, and what it takes to talk to them from a client.)

Also I read through the link you posted. There's a lot of truth to many of those frustrations, but a lot of it is based on misunderstandings of what Wayland actually is. Yes, most people should be using a toolkit. No, it's not great that the main choices are GTK and Qt. I think there's absolutely room for a mid-level toolkit that lets you do the basics without requiring all the Wayland boilerplate. smithay-client-toolkit is one such effort, and I think it's a good start, though something even higher-level on top of it would be nice.

I also don't get the callback hate. I much prefer registering callbacks over a ginormous switch statement that has to dispatch every event under the sun. Toolkits use callbacks too; does the author hate all toolkits as well? You actually could talk to a Wayland compositor with a big switch statement if you wanted, though you'd need to modify libwayland-client to return events as you iterate its event queue rather than dispatch things to callbacks. That could be a fun project for someone who wanted to make Wayland event handling just like libX11 event handling. (See: just a fundamental misunderstanding of what Wayland is.)

And comparing raylib to libwayland-client is silly; they're fundamentally different things. And you can use raylib to talk to a Wayland compositor. It's just a bad-faith argument.

If you want to compare libwayland-client to something, you have to compare it to libX11 or libxcb. And while yes, getting a simple window on-screen is indeed simpler with libX11/libxcb, doing anything more complicated than that is on par with what you'd end up doing with libwayland-client.

[0] Not counting the protocol code that wayland-scanner will generate for you, because that's like saying you have to count the lines of code in libX11 to write an X11 screenshot app.

guenthert 2026/03/23 17:56
MATE DE 在小型（在印度不算小）但不断增长（毫无疑问要归功于 Windows 11）的 Linux 桌面市场中相当受欢迎，不是吗？ 他们努力实现 Wayland 兼容性，但还没有完全实现（这是我从他们的发行说明中获取的；我自己现在在这里使用 Ubuntu/Mate 的古老版本）。

我不太了解 Waylands 的优点和缺点（只是当“性能”或“开销”被引用为放弃 X11 的理由时感到困惑，记住后者在 30 年前似乎并没有慢得难以忍受，而且从那时起计算机的性能，特别是计算机图形学的性能成倍增加）。 然而，有些人应该知道，但他们似乎并不那么兴奋： https://www.kicad.org/blog/2025/06/KiCad-and-Wayland-Support...

The MATE DE is fairly popular among the small (not so small in India) but growing (thanks to Windows 11 no doubt) Linux desktop market, isn't it? They strive for Wayland compatibility, but aren't quite there (such I take from their release notes; I myself use an ancient version of Ubuntu/Mate here, right now).

I'm not all that informed regarding Waylands benefits and shortcomings (just being puzzled when "performance" or "overhead" is quoted as reason to move away from X11, remembering that the latter didn't seem unbearable slow 30 years ago and that performance of computers in general and computer graphics in particular increased manifold since then). There are however some who should know, who don't seem all that excited: https://www.kicad.org/blog/2025/06/KiCad-and-Wayland-Support...

我正在寻找这样一行字：微软赞助了我们。即便如此，我也不明白他们为什么要在一个注定失败的项目上花费精力。我知道 Avalonia 作为一家小公司，在将 Avalonia UI 移植到 Wayland 之前还有一项艰巨的任务，这使得移植 MS 半废弃软件变得更加混乱。

corstian 2026/03/23 04:46
在 MAUI 和 Avalonia 之间，就技术质量和社区响应而言，Avalonia 是更优秀的框架。 Avalonia 没有的是 MAUI 拥有的企业组件库。作为这一举措的一部分，Avalonia 即将吸纳这些图书馆以及一大群 MAUI 团队。

换句话说; Avalonia 正在争夺 MAUI 的地盘。

Between MAUI and Avalonia, Avalonia is the superior framework when it comes to technical quality as well as community response. What Avalonia doesn't have is the enterprise component libraries MAUI has. As part of this move Avalonia is about to reel in these libraries, as well as a whole bunch of MAUI teams.

In other words; Avalonia is coming for MAUIs turf.

nobodyandproud 2026/03/23 03:47
> 但既然这些人不是白痴，我很高兴地认为我错过了一些东西。

微软政治。 请知情者确认，但我想说的是……

不同的组织争夺权力，您可以看到错误的组织和计划何时影响不同的产品。

我无法判断的是，是否有成熟的团队在争先恐后地保持相关性。或者，如果是新的团队和产品在不应该施加影响的地方施加影响。

但 Windows 团队不希望看到 Linux 受到关注，因此他们将尽自己的一份力量来阻碍任何操作系统 shim 或 Office 中的任何本机优先功能。

Office 组织希望扩展到 Windows 之外，但出于政治原因，唯一没有平台锁定的加载项技术是 JS，因此他们与 Azure/云团队结盟，允许第三方创建加载项。

由于这种合作关系，发行商需要了解 Entra 和合作伙伴中心的全部复杂性，而不是创建一个简化的插件商店。

我想象 UX 和 .NET 组织陷入了类似的政治斗争；但没有任何直接收入或产品的影响。

如果我不得不猜测的话，他们曾经是 Windows 团队的一员；现在他们已经加入了 Windows 团队。但由于独立于平台的举措（好），过去 20 多年来，对于桌面开发人员来说，这就是一场狗屎秀（坏）。

> But since these people aren't idiots, I gladly assume I am missing something.

Microsoft politics. Someone who’s aware please confirm but I want to say it’s something like…

Different orgs jockey for power and you can see when the wrong orgs and initiatives influence different products.

What I can’t tell is whether it’s established teams scrambling to stay relevant. Or if it’s new teams and products imposing their influence where they shouldn’t.

But the Windows team doesn’t want to see Linux get traction, so they’ll do their part to hamper any OS shims or any native-first functions in Office.

The Office org wants to expand beyond Windows but for political reasons, the only add-in tech without platform lock-in is JS so they ally with the Azure/Cloud team to allow third parties to create add-ins.

Because of this partnership, rather than making a streamlined add-in store, publishers are required to learn the full complexities of Entra and the Partner centers.

I imagine the UX and .NET orgs are caught in similar political battles; but without any direct income or product to influence.

If I had to guess, they were in the Windows team at one point; but with the platform-independent initiatives (good) it’s been a shitshow over the past 20+ years for desktop developers (bad).

pjmlp 2026/03/23 17:37
.NET属于DevDiv，隶属于Azure和AI。

请注意 DevDiv 现在支持多少种语言，请参阅语言下拉列表 https://devblogs.microsoft.com。

因此，即使 .NET 也必须争取内部相关性，Azure 对 CNCF 的大部分贡献都是 Go 和 Rust，他们甚至有一个“Rust for C#/.NET Developers”（https://microsoft.github.io/rust-for-dotnet-devs/latest） 教程。

如今，Azure 也主要采用 Azure Linux，即使 Azure 主机操作系统仍然基于 Windows。

.NET belongs to DevDiv, which is under Azure and AI.

Notice how many languages DevDiv supports nowadays, see languages dropdown on https://devblogs.microsoft.com.

Thus even .NET has to fight for internal relevance, most Azure contributions to CNCF are in Go and Rust, they even have a "Rust for C#/.NET Developers" (https://microsoft.github.io/rust-for-dotnet-devs/latest) tutorial.

Azure is also mostly doing Azure Linux nowadays, even if Azure Host OS is still Windows based.

array_key_first 2026/03/23 05:50

MS doesn't believe in any of their toolkits, and the source is their actions. First off, they're addicting to introducing new toolkits instead of improving existing ones. But that doesn't even matter, because they just use Electron anyway.
在 HN 中查看
exceptione 2026/03/23 04:17
MS有多重性格，所以有些人可能会这样做，我会给你的。与此同时，WPF 正在得到恢复。似乎不仅仅是普通开发人员得出这样的结论：自 wpf 以来的所有其他 UI 框架都是半生不熟的。比我更参与的人也做出了同样的评估： https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47480056

我建议大家忽略所有实验，直接使用 AvaloniaUI，因为它与 wpf 非常相似，积极开发且跨平台。我认为唯一的缺点是 Wayland 仍在开发中。

MS has multiple personalities, so some might do, I will give you that. Meanwhile, WPF is getting rehabilitated. It seems like that not only the average developer has concluded that all the other UI frameworks since wpf are half-baked. Someone more involved than me makes the same assessment: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47480056

I recommend everyone to ignore all experiments, and go straight for AvaloniaUI, as it is quite similar to wpf, actively devloped and cross-platform. The only downside I see is that Wayland is still in progress yet.

MikeCodesDotNET 2026/03/23 04:21
这是第一个预览版本。它的目标是 .NET 11 的预览版，这应该可以帮助您了解它目前不打算在生产中使用。

我们预计该预览版要到 11 月才会结束。有足够的时间来解决可访问性问题。

This is the first preview release. It’s targeting a preview of .NET 11, which should help you understand that it’s not intended to be used in production right now.

We don’t expect this to graduate from a preview until November. There’s plenty of time to sort out Accessibility.

siwatanejo 2026/03/23 03:48

Almost nobody needs accessibility; let's be realistic, it's obviously not a priority. The priority is to put this out the door (MVP style).
在 HN 中查看
keraf 2026/03/23 20:43
遗憾的是，可访问性常常处于最底层，因为开发人员大多不关心。然而，如果您在开始构建时就考虑到这一点，那么在大多数现代系统中实施起来相对简单。同样，事后才将 i18n 添加到应用程序比从一开始就添加 i18n 需要付出更多的努力。

可访问性不仅仅意味着残疾人可以轻松访问您的应用程序。辅助功能还使患有暂时性或情境性障碍的人能够更轻松地使用您的软件。我想到的一个简单的例子是单指缩放地图应用程序，只需点击一次，然后按住向上/向下滑动即可放大/缩小，这使得单手使用变得很容易。我知道很难与患有永久性残疾的人建立联系，但我们绝对可以与那些我们希望用一只手更容易使用某些东西的人建立联系，因为另一只手很忙。现在想象一下这是否是您可以使用它的唯一方式:-)

最终，它不会遗漏任何人，并使所有人受益。

Sadly accessibility often lands at the bottom of priorities because developers mostly don't care. Yet, it's relatively straight forward to implement in most modern systems if you start building it with that in mind. Similarly, adding i18n to an application as an afterthought requires more effort than if you would have done it from the start.

Accessibility doesn't only mean easing access to your application to people with disabilities. Accessibility makes it also easier for people with temporary or situational impairment to use your software. A simple example that comes to mind is single finger zooming on map applications, where you tap once and then hold-swipe up/down to zoom in/out, which makes it easy to be used with one hand. I know it's difficult to relate to people with permanent disabilities, but we can definitely relate to situational ones where we wish something was easier to use with one hand because the other was busy. Now imagine if it's the only way you can ever use it :-)

Ultimately, it leaves out no one and benefits everyone.

在 HN 中查看
skeeter2020 2026/03/23 04:17

skeeter2020 2026/03/23 04:19
如果他们的意思是“只有一小部分用户需要辅助功能支持”，这可能是正确的，但在过去 20 多年里，我没有在一家销售不需要提供支持的软件的组织工作过，而这些组织是 .net 跨平台 UI 解决方案的受众，因此在这种情况下他们是错误的；几乎每个人都“需要无障碍支持”。
If they mean "only a small subset of your users need accessibility support" this might be true, but I haven't worked for a organization selling software in the past 20+ years that hasn't needed to provide support, and those orgs are the audience for a .net cross-platform UI solution, so in that case they are wrong; almost everyone "needs accessibility support".
在 HN 中查看
saidnooneever 2026/03/23 04:24
提供产品支持和可访问性确实是不同的事情。

可访问性就像实施盲文以及聋哑人和色盲人士的东西等。

支持包括重置密码和帮助处理帐户等。

因此，一个是让特定类别的用户能够在一般意义上访问您的网站。另一个（支持）是为了帮助那些已经可以访问您的网站或服务的人。

provide support on a product and accessibility are really different things.

accessibility is like implementing braille and things for deaf and colourblind etc.

support is resetting password and helping with accounts etc.

so one is to get a certain category of users to be able to access your site in the general sense. the other (support) is about helping people who already can access your site or service.

在 HN 中查看
tacticus 2026/03/23 06:07
> 可访问性就像实施盲文以及聋人和色盲等的东西。

或者

- 更大的字体

- 更好的对比度控制，

- 非抽象艺术图像，

- 更大的按钮和键盘导航，

- 了解色盲有多种类型，有不同的要求，

- 由于散光患者阅读文本时出现问题，可以在您的应用程序和网站上设置灯光模式，

- 减少动画或运动模糊的数量

可访问性的范围并不小，其中一些是绝大多数产品所需要的。此外，可访问性要求也会随着时间而变化。眼睛和听力下降。浪费精力试图找到一些没有边框、几乎没有对比度来指示它所在位置的时尚按钮的愿望消失了

> accessibility is like implementing braille and things for deaf and colourblind etc.

or

- larger fonts

- Better contrast controls,

- Non abstract art iconography,

- larger buttons and keyboard navigation,

- understanding that there are many types of colourblindness with different requriements,

- the ability to set lightmode on your app and website due to the issues reading text for anyone with astigmatisms,

- reducing the amount of animation or motion blur

The range of what accessibility is isn't small and some of it is going to be required for the vast majority of products. Also accessibility requirements change over time. eyes and hearing degrade. the desire to waste energy trying to find some stylish button that has no border and almost no contrast to indicate where it is goes away

pjmlp 2026/03/23 02:26
从 Xamarin.Forms 重写到 MAUI 给社区中的许多人带来了不好的感受，并且对 Avalonia 使其在 GNU/Linux 上实现感到荣幸。

顺便说一句，在 macOS 上，MAUI 使用 Catalyst 作为后端，而不是本机 macOS API。

另外有趣的是，Miguel de Icaza 如今完全切换到 Swift 生态系统，并负责让 Godot 在 iPad 上开发游戏成为现实。或者将他的旧 .NET 想法移植到 Swift 中。

The rewrite from Xamarin.Forms into MAUI, has given a bad taste to many in the community, and kudos to Avalonia to make it happen on GNU/Linux.

By the way on macOS MAUI uses Catalyst as backend, not native macOS APIs.

Also it is kind of interesting that Miguel de Icaza, nowadays completely switched into Swift ecosystem, and is the responsible for making game development on iPad with Godot a reality. Or porting old .NET ideas of his into Swift.

在 HN 中查看
fingerlocks 2026/03/23 07:10
> 顺便说一句，在 macOS 上 MAUI 使用 Catalyst 作为后端，而不是本机 macOS API。

这意味着什么？ Mac Catalyst 是原生的。它只是 iPhone 的 UIKit 和 MacOS 上的 AppKit 之间的一座薄桥，而这实际上是整个庞大的 Apple SDK 中仅有的两个不同的框架。

> By the way on macOS MAUI uses Catalyst as backend, not native macOS APIs.

What does this mean? Mac Catalyst is native. It’s just a thin bridge between iPhone’s UIKit and AppKit on MacOS, which are really the only two divergent frameworks in the entirety of the massive Apple SDK.

在 HN 中查看
flohofwoe 2026/03/23 20:24
这仍然是非常奇怪的。 iOS UIKit 和 macOS AppKit 之间的差异非常小，并且已经为“跨平台协同”而设计，例如使用几乎相同的代码为 macOS 和 iOS 提供服务。
It's still extremely weird. The difference between iOS UIKit and macOS AppKit is extremely small and already designed for 'cross-platform synergy', e.g. serving both macOS and iOS with mostly the same code.
在 HN 中查看
pjmlp 2026/03/23 14:59
它实际上并不相同，正是因为这种差异以及 Catalyst 未涵盖的平台差异。

此外，它们并不是唯一有分歧的框架，尽管这是另一个讨论。

It isn't really the same, exactly because of that divergence, and platform differences not covered by Catalyst.

Also they aren't the only frameworks that are divergent, although that is another discussion.

fingerlocks 2026/03/23 16:47

不仅仅是因为在为 MacO 编译 CoreLocation 时罗盘被#ifdef 掉了，也不算“发散”

Let’s have that discussion. I’m all ears. Can you name any other pair of frameworks that provide identical functionality, but they are divergent such that each framework is mutually exclusive to its target platform?

And no just because the compass is #ifdef’d out when CoreLocation is compiled for MacOs does not count as “divergent”

rafram 2026/03/23 19:58
OpenGL 仍然受到支持，并且 Metal 已经存在 12 年了。

Cocoa Touch 只是包括 UIKit 在内的 iOS 框架的总称，Catalyst 也使用 UIKit。

OpenGL is still supported, and Metal has been around for 12 years.

Cocoa Touch is just the umbrella term for iOS frameworks including UIKit, and Catalyst also uses UIKit.

在 HN 中查看
pjmlp 2026/03/23 20:22
OpenGL 并没有得到真正的支持，它对于现有的代码来说是一瘸一拐的，现在根据操作系统版本，它实际上有 Metal 底层，但不是 Metal 能够用硬件做的事情。
OpenGL isn't really supported, it limps along for existing code, nowadays depending on the OS version, it actually has Metal underneath, but not what Metal is capable of doing with the hardware.

exceptione 2026/03/23 02:33
让我重新表述一下兄弟所说的话：当您已经获得了现有的传统仅 Windows WPF 应用程序并且您希望该应用程序跨平台时，付费产品适合您 按原样，放弃将其移植到 AvaloniaUI 的任何努力。

如果您直接在 AvaloniaUI 中构建您的东西，您将不需要付费产品。

Let me rephrase what sibling said: the paid offering is for you when you have gotten an existing traditional windows-only wpf application and you want to have that appplication cross-platform as-is, foregoing any effort to port it to AvaloniaUI.

You won't need the paid offering if you build your stuff in AvaloniaUI directly.

exceptione 2026/03/23 03:00
我无法具体评论，但它与 MVVM 扩展工具包配合使用，可以方便地解耦事件处理，并且在复杂场景中很有帮助。

恕我直言，最重要的是寻找您需要的组件。您可以自己构建，但如果您可以使用现成的东西，那当然会有所帮助。您最好看看他们的画廊，看看是否看到类似的东西可以满足您的需求。

I can't comment on that specifically, but it works with MVVM extensions toolkit, which is handy for decoupling of event handling and is helpful in complex scenario's.

Most import thing to look for are the components you need imho. You can build themselves, but if you can use something ready made, that helps of course. You would best take look at their gallery to see if you see something similar for your needs.

Kwpolska 2026/03/23 02:17
Avalonia 是免费且开源的。 Avalonia MAUI 目前似乎也是 MIT [0]。您链接到的定价是其付费产品的定价，其中包含 Avalonia 和 WPF 风格的 API，以便轻松迁移遗留应用程序。

[0] https://github.com/AvaloniaUI/Avalonia.Controls.Maui/blob/ma...

Avalonia is free and open-source. Avalonia MAUI currently appears to be MIT as well [0]. The pricing you’ve linked to is pricing for their paid offering, which wraps Avalonia and a WPF-style API for easy migration of legacy apps.

[0] https://github.com/AvaloniaUI/Avalonia.Controls.Maui/blob/ma...

liamgm 2026/03/23 16:40
avalonia xpf 是 avalonia 之上的 xplat wpf 运行时，付费。 avalonia maui 目前是 mit avalonia 上的 xplat maui 运行时。 avalonia 有他们自己的 ui，称为 avaloniaUI 也是 xplat，免费。
avalonia xpf is xplat wpf runtime on top avalonia, paid. avalonia maui is xplat maui runtime on top avalonia, mit for now. avalonia have they own ui called avaloniaUI also xplat, free.

Drastically less likely to get a rug pull, licensing wouldn't really be an issue for any commercial project, and easy to sell to an employer.
在 HN 中查看
Rohansi 2026/03/23 11:13
> 被拉扯的可能性大大降低

微软废弃的 UI 库列表却另有说法。

> 对于任何商业项目来说，许可并不是真正的问题

它获得了麻省理工学院的许可。商业用途比Qt更简单。

> 容易卖给雇主

这是一项艰难的任务。由于某种原因，雇主对 .NET 领域中的非 Microsoft 依赖性感到奇怪。对我个人来说这没有任何意义。对于网络或移动应用程序来说，它们可能没有那么严格。但如果你的雇主是这样的，那肯定会破坏交易。

> Drastically less likely to get a rug pull

Microsoft's list of abandoned UI libraries says otherwise.

> licensing wouldn't really be an issue for any commercial project

It's MIT licensed. Simpler for commercial use than Qt.

> easy to sell to an employer

That's a tough one. For some reason employers are weird about non-Microsoft dependencies in the .NET space. It makes no sense to me personally. They likely aren't nearly as strict for web or mobile apps. But that'll definitely be a dealbreaker if your employer is like that.

在 HN 中查看
giancarlostoro 2026/03/23 11:53
> 这是一项艰难的任务。由于某种原因，雇主对 .NET 领域中的非 Microsoft 依赖性感到奇怪。对我个人来说这没有任何意义。对于网络或移动应用程序来说，它们可能没有那么严格。但如果你的雇主是这样的，那肯定会破坏交易。

我们有一个供应商从“此许可证完全免版税”或其他什么，到我们需要向您组织中的所有开发人员收费，然后，对于您应用程序的每个用户，我们都希望获得报酬。荒谬的。问题是，当您使用许可证的项目可能根本无法带来收入时，这些公司开始渴望获得许可成本，并想榨干您的每一块钱。

> That's a tough one. For some reason employers are weird about non-Microsoft dependencies in the .NET space. It makes no sense to me personally. They likely aren't nearly as strict for web or mobile apps. But that'll definitely be a dealbreaker if your employer is like that.

We had a vendor go from "this license is fully royalty free" or whatever, to, we need to charge for all devs in your org, to then, for every single user of your app, we want to be paid. Ridiculous. The problem is companies who start getting license cost hungry and wanna squeeze every dollar out of you, when the project you're using it on might not even bring revenue.

orthoxerox 2026/03/23 17:28
> 出于某种原因，雇主对 .NET 领域中的非 Microsoft 依赖性感到奇怪。

UI 工具包可能是第三方供应商可以挑战 MS 的唯一领域。他们可以直接询问首席技术官，他或她能说出多少个不受支持或支持不佳的 Microsoft 框架。

> For some reason employers are weird about non-Microsoft dependencies in the .NET space.

UI toolkits are probably the only space where a third-party vendor can challenge the MS. They can just ask the CTO how many unsupported or badly supported frameworks from Microsoft he or she can name.

ChicagoDave 2026/03/23 05:14
我一直在使用 Claude 构建几个应用程序的本机版本，曾经不可想象的事情（维护多个代码库）现在变得相当微不足道。 Electron/Tauri 的实现质量很高。

我不确定像毛伊岛这样的平台是否还有必要。

我确实注意到“如果你不想要液体玻璃”这一评论是对 GenAI 原生开发的直接回应。

时间会证明一切。

I’ve been using Claude to build native versions of a couple of apps and what was once unthinkable (maintaining multiple code bases) is now fairly trivial. And Electron/Tauri implementations are high quality.

I’m not sure platforms like Maui are necessary anymore.

I did note the comment “if you don’t want Liquid Glass” as a direct response to GenAI native development.

Time will tell.

LeFantome 2026/03/23 02:17
他们所说的MAUI就是你引用的微软框架。

MAUI 是开源的，但 Microsoft 不提供 Linux 后端。这是非 Microsoft 为将 Linux 支持引入 MAUI 所做的努力。

The MAUI they are talking about is the Microsoft framework you are referencing.

MAUI is Open Source but Microsoft does not provide a Linux back-end. This is a non-Microsoft effort to bring Linux support to MAUI.

drzaiusx11 2026/03/23 05:49
C# 是跨平台的，我敢打赌现在大多数 .Net 服务都在 Linux 上运行（毕竟 Azure 运行的 Linux VM 比 Windows VM 更多）这恰好填补了客户端空白，因此您可以将整个堆栈统一在一种语言、一个节点等下
C# is cross platform, I'd bet money that most .Net services run on Linux these days (Azure runs more Linux VMs than Windows VMs after all) This just fills the client side gap so you can unify the full stack under one language a la node etc

jeremycarter 2026/03/23 05:54

Hundreds of thousands of .NET applications run inside Linux Docker containers.
